meta.notes: Working tracker dataset. Public ABS/RBA series are the primary maintenance baseline, with PropTrack, Domain and SQM retained as optional market overlays.

quality_rules.fresh: Latest observation is within the expected release lag for its frequency.
quality_rules.watch: Series is usable but needs review because the source changed, has a manual dependency, or is nearing its expected update date.
quality_rules.stale: Latest observation is older than expected for its frequency.
quality_rules.discontinued: Source has ceased or changed methodology; preserve history but do not treat it as a live feed.

source_registry.watch: Home value / home price indexes, median values, city momentum
source_registry.update_cadence: Monthly or quarterly, depending on source

source_registry.watch: Auction clearance, auction volumes, listings, days on market, vendor discounting

primary_sources.name: Ray White Economics and auction reports

source_registry.update_cadence: Weekly for auctions; monthly for listings

source_registry.watch: Building approvals, completions, commencements, dwelling stock, construction constraints

primary_sources.name: National Housing Supply and Affordability Council

source_registry.update_cadence: Monthly for approvals; quarterly for activity/completions; annual for NHSAC system report

source_registry.watch: New loan commitments, investor share, first-home buyer share, serviceability and risk settings

primary_sources.name: RBA financial aggregates and lending rates

source_registry.update_cadence: Monthly for ABS/RBA; quarterly for APRA
source_registry.scope: National, with state splits where available

source_registry.watch: Vacancy rates, advertised rents, rental yields, rental listings
source_registry.update_cadence: Monthly or quarterly, depending on source

source_registry.watch: Cash rate, inflation, labour market, wages, national accounts, population growth

primary_sources.name: RBA cash rate target and monetary policy statements
ABS Consumer Price Index / monthly CPI
ABS National, state and territory population

source_registry.update_cadence: Monthly, quarterly, and RBA meeting cycle

source_registry.watch: Macroprudential rules, first-home buyer schemes, stamp duty/land tax, planning and supply reform

primary_sources.name: Revenue NSW first-home buyer and transfer duty settings
Victorian State Revenue Office property grants and duties
Queensland Revenue Office property concessions and grants
WA transfer duty and first home owner grant settings

source_registry.update_cadence: Ad hoc when policy changes; review monthly
source_registry.scope: National plus NSW, VIC, QLD, WA

source_registry.watch: Mean and median household income, wages, disposable income, dwelling-price-to-income and repayment burdens
source_registry.update_cadence: Quarterly for wages; periodic survey and five-year Census for household income
source_registry.scope: National and state where survey quality supports publication

source_registry.watch: Total returns, valuation and earnings signals for residential developers, housing platforms, lenders and A-REITs
source_registry.update_cadence: Daily prices; quarterly market-cap rebalance; reporting-cycle fundamentals
source_registry.scope: Australian listed securities; separate residential-exposure basket recommended

home_value_index_mom.label: Home value growth, month-on-month
home_value_index_mom.definition: Monthly percentage change in the residential property price index.
home_value_index_mom.usage: Use it to identify short-run price momentum and early turning points before annual growth rates respond.
home_value_index_mom.housing_market_link: Positive readings indicate rising dwelling values; persistent acceleration can signal stronger demand relative to available supply.
home_value_index_mom.status_note: Subscription-dependent source for full monthly maintenance workflow. Keep as optional overlay while core monitoring uses public ABS/RBA series.

building_approvals_total_dwellings.label: Total dwellings approved (seasonally adjusted)
building_approvals_total_dwellings.definition: Number of dwelling units approved for construction, adjusted for recurring seasonal patterns.
building_approvals_total_dwellings.usage: Use it as a leading indicator of the residential construction pipeline, while allowing for projects that are delayed or never commence.
building_approvals_total_dwellings.housing_market_link: A sustained rise can add future housing supply and ease shortages; weak approvals can prolong supply constraints and housing-cost pressure.
building_approvals_total_dwellings.status_note: Latest parsed ABS seasonally adjusted dwelling approvals value is 17019.

building_approvals_total_dwellings_state.label: Dwelling approvals (Australia total; state proxies for cities)
building_approvals_total_dwellings_state.definition: Seasonally adjusted dwelling approvals: the National view is the Australia total, while Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane and Perth display NSW, Victoria, Queensland and WA respectively.
building_approvals_total_dwellings_state.usage: Use the National view for the aggregate construction pipeline and the city tabs for directional state comparisons; the city-labelled observations are not capital-city-only counts.
building_approvals_total_dwellings_state.housing_market_link: Approvals lead potential new supply, but not every approval commences and state totals can differ materially from capital-city conditions.
building_approvals_total_dwellings_state.status_note: National is the Australia total. City labels are state proxies: Sydney→NSW, Melbourne→VIC, Brisbane→QLD, Perth→WA.

cash_rate.label: RBA cash rate target
cash_rate.definition: The Reserve Bank of Australia's target for the overnight cash rate.
cash_rate.usage: Use it as the primary signal of monetary-policy stance and the direction of economy-wide borrowing costs.
cash_rate.housing_market_link: Cash-rate changes flow into mortgage pricing, borrowing capacity and repayments, affecting buyer demand, refinancing stress and property values.
cash_rate.status_note: Latest parsed RBA cash rate target is 4.35%.

housing_lending_rate_owner_occupier_vari.label: Housing lending rate (owner-occupier variable, all institutions)
housing_lending_rate_owner_occupier_vari.definition: Average variable interest rate on outstanding owner-occupier housing loans across lending institutions.
housing_lending_rate_owner_occupier_vari.usage: Use it to track the financing cost households are actually carrying, rather than relying only on the policy rate.
housing_lending_rate_owner_occupier_vari.housing_market_link: Higher mortgage rates reduce serviceability and purchasing power; lower rates can support transaction volumes and prices.
housing_lending_rate_owner_occupier_vari.status_note: Latest parsed RBA F6 owner-occupier outstanding variable housing lending rate (all institutions) is 6.2%.

unemployment_rate.label: Unemployment rate, seasonally adjusted
unemployment_rate.definition: Share of the labour force that is without work, available to work and actively seeking employment.
unemployment_rate.usage: Use it to assess labour-market slack, household income security and cyclical downside risk.
unemployment_rate.housing_market_link: Rising unemployment can weaken buyer demand and increase arrears or forced-sale risk; low unemployment generally supports repayments and housing demand.
unemployment_rate.status_note: Latest parsed ABS seasonally adjusted unemployment rate is 4.4%.

employed_people.label: Employed people, seasonally adjusted
employed_people.definition: Total number of employed people, seasonally adjusted and reported in thousands.
employed_people.usage: Use it alongside unemployment to distinguish genuine job creation from changes in labour-force participation.
employed_people.housing_market_link: Employment growth supports household formation, mortgage capacity and rental demand, particularly where jobs attract interstate or overseas migration.
employed_people.status_note: Latest parsed ABS seasonally adjusted employed people is 14738.8 ('000 persons).

cpi_trimmed_mean_yoy.label: Trimmed mean inflation, year-on-year
cpi_trimmed_mean_yoy.definition: Underlying annual inflation measure that removes the largest price rises and falls each period.
cpi_trimmed_mean_yoy.usage: Use it to judge persistent inflation pressure and the likely direction of RBA policy beyond volatile headline movements.
cpi_trimmed_mean_yoy.housing_market_link: Persistent underlying inflation can keep interest rates higher for longer, while also feeding construction, maintenance and rental costs.
cpi_trimmed_mean_yoy.status_note: Full available RBA G1 quarterly trimmed mean inflation history joined to the ABS complete monthly CPI publication at its first available recent observation.
cpi_trimmed_mean_yoy.methodology_note: Quarterly RBA G1 history is joined to the ABS complete monthly CPI series. Treat the frequency transition as a documented series break when reading cycles.

cpi_headline_yoy.label: Headline CPI inflation, year-on-year
cpi_headline_yoy.definition: Annual percentage change in the price of the household consumption basket measured by the Consumer Price Index.
cpi_headline_yoy.usage: Use it to track cost-of-living pressure, real household income and the broad inflation environment.
cpi_headline_yoy.housing_market_link: High inflation can squeeze deposit saving and repayment capacity, lift building costs and prompt tighter monetary policy that weighs on housing demand.
cpi_headline_yoy.status_note: Full available RBA G1 quarterly annual inflation history joined to the ABS complete monthly CPI publication at its first available recent observation.
cpi_headline_yoy.methodology_note: Quarterly RBA G1 history is joined to the ABS complete monthly CPI series. Treat the frequency transition as a documented series break when reading cycles.

lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_q.label: New loan commitments for dwellings, quarter-on-quarter
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_q.definition: Quarterly percentage change in the seasonally adjusted number of new dwelling loan commitments.
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_q.usage: Use it as a short-run momentum measure for funded housing demand; refer to the level series to distinguish growth from scale.
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_q.housing_market_link: Accelerating commitments often accompany stronger turnover and buyer competition; contraction can foreshadow softer transactions and price momentum.
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_q.status_note: Quarter-on-quarter change calculated from the ABS seasonally adjusted total number of new dwelling loan commitments; comparable total series begins in September quarter 2019.

vacancy_rate.label: Rental vacancy rate
vacancy_rate.definition: Percentage of the rental dwelling stock that is advertised as vacant or otherwise available for lease.
vacancy_rate.usage: Use it to measure rental-market tightness and the balance between available properties and tenant demand.
vacancy_rate.housing_market_link: Low vacancy rates tend to support faster rent growth and investor returns, while high vacancy rates indicate more tenant choice and weaker rental pressure.
vacancy_rate.status_note: Public headline figures available, but collection is still manual. Next step is adding repeatable monthly extraction for national and key capitals.

lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_n.label: New dwelling loan commitments (number)
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_n.definition: Number of borrower-accepted new dwelling loan commitments, seasonally adjusted and excluding refinancing.
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_n.usage: Use it to track the volume of financed housing transactions without the direct effect of changing dwelling prices or average loan sizes.
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_n.housing_market_link: Rising commitments generally signal stronger funded buyer demand and can lead settlement and turnover activity.
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_n.status_note: ABS seasonally adjusted total number of borrower-accepted new dwelling loan commitments; excludes refinancing and the comparable total series begins in September quarter 2019.

lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_v.label: New dwelling loan commitments (value)
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_v.definition: Dollar value of borrower-accepted new dwelling loan commitments, seasonally adjusted and excluding refinancing.
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_v.usage: Use it to measure the flow of new housing credit, while recognising that it reflects both transaction volumes and average loan size.
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_v.housing_market_link: Credit-flow growth can support purchasing capacity and prices; divergence from commitment counts often reflects changing prices or borrower mix.
lending_new_loan_commitments_dwellings_v.status_note: ABS seasonally adjusted dollar value of borrower-accepted new dwelling loan commitments; excludes refinancing.

asx_200.label: S&P/ASX 200
asx_200.usage: Use it as a broad Australian risk-sentiment and listed-economy indicator.
asx_200.housing_market_link: Equity strength can accompany improving confidence and wealth, but the index is not a direct housing-price measure.
asx_200.status_note: Monthly closing index level retrieved via Yahoo Finance; latest observation is 2026-07.
asx_200.methodology_note: Monthly close; price-return index unless otherwise stated. Values may be revised by the data provider.

sp_500.label: S&P 500
sp_500.usage: Use it to track global risk appetite and US financial conditions.
sp_500.housing_market_link: Large global market moves can affect Australian funding conditions, confidence and household portfolios.
sp_500.status_note: Monthly closing index level retrieved via Yahoo Finance; latest observation is 2026-07.
sp_500.methodology_note: Monthly close; price-return index unless otherwise stated. Values may be revised by the data provider.

msci_acwi.label: MSCI ACWI (USD, price return)
msci_acwi.definition: Global developed and emerging-market equity performance in US dollars.
msci_acwi.usage: Use it as a broad global risk-appetite benchmark.
msci_acwi.housing_market_link: Global risk cycles can influence capital flows, funding costs and Australian household wealth.
msci_acwi.status_note: Monthly closing index level retrieved via Yahoo Finance; latest observation is 2026-07.
msci_acwi.methodology_note: Monthly close; price-return index unless otherwise stated. Values may be revised by the data provider.

asx_200_real_estate.label: S&P/ASX 200 Real Estate Index
asx_200_real_estate.definition: Price-return index for S&P/ASX 200 companies classified in the GICS real-estate sector.
asx_200_real_estate.usage: Use it to monitor listed-market expectations for Australian real-estate businesses.
asx_200_real_estate.housing_market_link: It is a liquid sentiment indicator, but includes commercial property and does not directly measure dwelling values.
asx_200_real_estate.status_note: Monthly closing index level retrieved via Yahoo Finance; latest observation is 2026-07.
asx_200_real_estate.methodology_note: Monthly close; price-return index unless otherwise stated. Values may be revised by the data provider.

asx_200_areit.label: S&P/ASX 200 A-REIT Index
asx_200_areit.definition: Price-return index for listed vehicles classified as Australian real-estate investment trusts.
asx_200_areit.usage: Use it to track listed property valuations and sensitivity to bond yields and funding costs.
asx_200_areit.housing_market_link: A-REIT performance is mostly commercial-property exposure and should not be read as a residential-price index.
asx_200_areit.status_note: Monthly closing index level retrieved via Yahoo Finance; latest observation is 2026-07.
asx_200_areit.methodology_note: Monthly close; price-return index unless otherwise stated. Values may be revised by the data provider.

us_treasury_3y.label: US Treasury 3-year yield
us_treasury_3y.usage: Use it to monitor medium-term US policy and growth expectations.
us_treasury_3y.housing_market_link: US yields influence global funding costs and can flow through to Australian wholesale funding and mortgage pricing.
us_treasury_3y.source: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED DGS3)
us_treasury_3y.status_note: Last available daily FRED observation in each month; latest month-end value is 4.2%.

us_treasury_10y.label: US Treasury 10-year yield
us_treasury_10y.usage: Use it as a global long-term discount-rate and inflation-expectations indicator.
us_treasury_10y.housing_market_link: Higher global long yields can pressure bank funding, fixed mortgage rates and listed property valuations.
us_treasury_10y.source: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED DGS10)
us_treasury_10y.status_note: Last available daily FRED observation in each month; latest month-end value is 4.57%.

wage_growth_private_yoy.label: Private-sector wage growth, year-on-year
wage_growth_private_yoy.definition: Annual growth in private-sector hourly rates of pay excluding bonuses, seasonally adjusted.
wage_growth_private_yoy.usage: Use it to assess household purchasing-power growth and labour-cost pressure in the private economy.
wage_growth_private_yoy.housing_market_link: Income growth supports borrowing capacity and repayments, while persistent wage pressure can also keep interest rates higher.
wage_growth_private_yoy.status_note: ABS annual private-sector wage growth, seasonally adjusted; latest observation is 3.2%.

wage_growth_public_yoy.label: Public-sector wage growth, year-on-year
wage_growth_public_yoy.definition: Annual growth in public-sector hourly rates of pay excluding bonuses, seasonally adjusted.
wage_growth_public_yoy.usage: Use it alongside private wages to identify sector differences in household income momentum.
wage_growth_public_yoy.housing_market_link: Public-sector wages support incomes in government-heavy regions and contribute to aggregate serviceability and demand.
wage_growth_public_yoy.status_note: ABS annual public-sector wage growth, seasonally adjusted; latest observation is 3.3%.

average_weekly_earnings_private.label: Private-sector average weekly ordinary earnings
average_weekly_earnings_private.definition: Average weekly ordinary time earnings for private-sector full-time adults, persons, in current dollars and before tax; original series.
average_weekly_earnings_private.usage: Use it as a dollar-level earnings measure. Compare it with WPI because changes can also reflect shifts in workforce composition.
average_weekly_earnings_private.housing_market_link: Higher weekly earnings generally increase borrowing capacity and repayment resilience, although averages do not describe the income distribution.
average_weekly_earnings_private.status_note: ABS original full-time adult average weekly ordinary time earnings for the private sector; latest value is $2,006.00 per week.
average_weekly_earnings_private.methodology_note: Original six-monthly average. It is affected by workforce composition and is not directly comparable with the fixed-job Wage Price Index.

average_weekly_earnings_public.label: Public-sector average weekly ordinary earnings
average_weekly_earnings_public.definition: Average weekly ordinary time earnings for public-sector full-time adults, persons, in current dollars and before tax; original series.
average_weekly_earnings_public.usage: Use it to compare the dollar level of public-sector earnings with private-sector earnings, allowing for different occupational composition.
average_weekly_earnings_public.housing_market_link: Public-sector earnings can support housing demand in government-employment centres, but this average is not a typical-household income measure.
average_weekly_earnings_public.status_note: ABS original full-time adult average weekly ordinary time earnings for the public sector; latest value is $2,215.90 per week.
average_weekly_earnings_public.methodology_note: Original six-monthly average. It is affected by workforce composition and is not directly comparable with the fixed-job Wage Price Index.

real_cash_rate_trimmed_mean.label: Real cash rate (trimmed mean basis)
real_cash_rate_trimmed_mean.definition: An ex-post real policy-rate proxy, expressed as the cash rate less underlying annual inflation.
real_cash_rate_trimmed_mean.usage: Read positive values as restrictive in real terms and negative values as accommodative, while allowing for the backward-looking inflation denominator.
real_cash_rate_trimmed_mean.housing_market_link: Higher real policy rates generally weaken borrowing capacity and housing demand; easing real rates can improve financing conditions.
real_cash_rate_trimmed_mean.source: Calculated from Reserve Bank of Australia and ABS Consumer Price Index, Australia
real_cash_rate_trimmed_mean.calculation: RBA cash rate target minus annual trimmed mean CPI inflation
real_cash_rate_trimmed_mean.methodology_note: The cash rate is carried to each CPI observation period. This is an analytical proxy, not an expected real rate.

real_mortgage_rate_headline.label: Real variable mortgage rate
real_mortgage_rate_headline.definition: An ex-post real mortgage-rate proxy using the average outstanding owner-occupier variable lending rate.
real_mortgage_rate_headline.usage: Use it to compare nominal mortgage pricing with realised inflation, not as a direct household cash-flow measure.
real_mortgage_rate_headline.housing_market_link: A rising real mortgage rate increases the inflation-adjusted financing hurdle facing leveraged buyers and existing borrowers.
real_mortgage_rate_headline.source: Calculated from RBA Housing Lending Rates (F6) and ABS Consumer Price Index, Australia
real_mortgage_rate_headline.calculation: Owner-occupier variable mortgage rate minus annual headline CPI inflation
real_mortgage_rate_headline.methodology_note: The latest CPI observation at or before each mortgage-rate month is used. Borrower-specific rates differ from this system average.

mortgage_cash_spread.label: Variable mortgage rate spread to cash rate
mortgage_cash_spread.definition: The gap between the average outstanding variable owner-occupier mortgage rate and the policy cash rate.
mortgage_cash_spread.usage: Track whether lender pricing is amplifying or cushioning changes in the policy rate.
mortgage_cash_spread.housing_market_link: A wider spread tightens household financing conditions beyond the cash-rate setting; a narrower spread provides some offset.
mortgage_cash_spread.source: Calculated from RBA Housing Lending Rates (F6) and Reserve Bank of Australia
mortgage_cash_spread.calculation: Owner-occupier variable mortgage rate minus RBA cash rate target
mortgage_cash_spread.methodology_note: The latest cash-rate setting in each mortgage-rate month is used.

real_wage_growth_private.label: Real private-sector wage growth
real_wage_growth_private.definition: A purchasing-power proxy for private-sector wages after headline consumer-price inflation.
real_wage_growth_private.usage: Use the sign and direction to assess whether wage growth is gaining or losing ground against consumer prices.
real_wage_growth_private.housing_market_link: Sustained positive real wage growth can improve deposit accumulation and mortgage serviceability; negative growth can constrain both.
real_wage_growth_private.source: Calculated from ABS Wage Price Index, Australia and ABS Consumer Price Index, Australia
real_wage_growth_private.calculation: Annual private-sector Wage Price Index growth minus annual headline CPI inflation
real_wage_growth_private.methodology_note: The latest CPI observation at or before each wage quarter is used. This is a growth differential, not a household-income level.

real_wage_growth_public.label: Real public-sector wage growth
real_wage_growth_public.definition: A purchasing-power proxy for public-sector wages after headline consumer-price inflation.
real_wage_growth_public.usage: Use the sign and direction to assess whether wage growth is gaining or losing ground against consumer prices.
real_wage_growth_public.housing_market_link: Sustained positive real wage growth can improve deposit accumulation and mortgage serviceability; negative growth can constrain both.
real_wage_growth_public.source: Calculated from ABS Wage Price Index, Australia and ABS Consumer Price Index, Australia
real_wage_growth_public.calculation: Annual public-sector Wage Price Index growth minus annual headline CPI inflation
real_wage_growth_public.methodology_note: The latest CPI observation at or before each wage quarter is used. This is a growth differential, not a household-income level.

us_yield_curve_10y_3y.label: US Treasury 10-year minus 3-year spread
us_yield_curve_10y_3y.definition: A medium-to-long US yield-curve slope measure; negative readings indicate inversion between these maturities.
us_yield_curve_10y_3y.usage: Use it as a global growth and rate-cycle signal rather than a direct Australian mortgage-rate forecast.
us_yield_curve_10y_3y.housing_market_link: Global bond conditions influence Australian wholesale funding and longer-term discount rates, indirectly affecting lenders and property assets.
us_yield_curve_10y_3y.source: Calculated from Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED DGS10) and Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED DGS3)

component_sources.name: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED DGS10)
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED DGS3)

us_yield_curve_10y_3y.calculation: US 10-year Treasury yield minus US 3-year Treasury yield
us_yield_curve_10y_3y.methodology_note: Calculated from matched monthly FRED observations.

average_new_dwelling_loan_value.label: Implied average new dwelling loan commitment
average_new_dwelling_loan_value.definition: The implied average dollar value per new dwelling loan commitment in the ABS aggregate data.
average_new_dwelling_loan_value.usage: Use it to track changes in the typical financed amount, while remembering that shifts in borrower and dwelling mix also move the average.
average_new_dwelling_loan_value.housing_market_link: Rising average commitments may signal higher prices, larger deposits, stronger borrowing capacity, or a shift toward more expensive markets.
average_new_dwelling_loan_value.calculation: Total value of new dwelling loan commitments divided by total number of commitments
average_new_dwelling_loan_value.methodology_note: Aggregate value divided by aggregate count. It is not the ABS median loan size and excludes refinancing consistently with the inputs.

dwelling_approvals_yoy.label: Dwelling approvals annual growth
dwelling_approvals_yoy.definition: The annual growth rate of total seasonally adjusted Australian dwelling approvals.
dwelling_approvals_yoy.usage: Use it to identify acceleration or contraction in the prospective residential construction pipeline while looking through monthly volatility.
dwelling_approvals_yoy.housing_market_link: Stronger approvals growth points to a potentially larger future supply pipeline, although approvals do not always translate into commencements or completions.
dwelling_approvals_yoy.source: Calculated from ABS Building Approvals, Australia
dwelling_approvals_yoy.calculation: Percentage change in seasonally adjusted dwelling approvals from 12 months earlier
dwelling_approvals_yoy.methodology_note: Calculated only where an observation exists exactly 12 months earlier.

asx_real_estate_relative.label: ASX 200 Real Estate relative to ASX 200
asx_real_estate_relative.definition: A relative-price index showing whether listed real-estate exposure has outperformed or underperformed the broad Australian equity market.
asx_real_estate_relative.usage: Values above 100 indicate cumulative outperformance since the common start; focus on direction and cycles rather than the absolute level.
asx_real_estate_relative.housing_market_link: Relative performance captures market expectations for property earnings, funding costs and valuations, but listed exposures are not a direct measure of dwelling prices.
asx_real_estate_relative.source: Calculated from S&P/ASX 200 Real Estate Index and S&P/ASX 200
asx_real_estate_relative.calculation: Ratio of S&P/ASX 200 Real Estate Index to S&P/ASX 200, rebased to 100 at the first common month
asx_real_estate_relative.methodology_note: Monthly price-index levels are divided and rebased. Dividends are excluded where the component series is a price-return index.

asx_areit_relative.label: ASX 200 A-REIT relative to ASX 200
asx_areit_relative.definition: A relative-price index showing whether listed real-estate exposure has outperformed or underperformed the broad Australian equity market.
asx_areit_relative.usage: Values above 100 indicate cumulative outperformance since the common start; focus on direction and cycles rather than the absolute level.
asx_areit_relative.housing_market_link: Relative performance captures market expectations for property earnings, funding costs and valuations, but listed exposures are not a direct measure of dwelling prices.
asx_areit_relative.source: Calculated from S&P/ASX 200 A-REIT Index and S&P/ASX 200
asx_areit_relative.calculation: Ratio of S&P/ASX 200 A-REIT Index to S&P/ASX 200, rebased to 100 at the first common month
asx_areit_relative.methodology_note: Monthly price-index levels are divided and rebased. Dividends are excluded where the component series is a price-return index.
